description_long: This rifle features a compact, utilitarian design with a dark metallic receiver and barrel, complemented by a weathered, reddish-brown handguard. The stock is a minimalist, skeletonized wire frame, likely designed for reduced weight and easy maneuverability in urban environments. The magazine is a large, box-type, protruding from the bottom, suggesting a high-capacity, detachable design. The pistol grip is ergonomically shaped and appears to be made of a different, possibly polymer material for comfort. The barrel is short and ends with a perforated muzzle device, likely a flash hider or compensator. Iron sights are mounted on top, with no visible optics or advanced sighting systems.
description: A compact, dark metal rifle with a skeletonized wire stock, box magazine, and a short barrel with a perforated muzzle device. The handguard is worn and reddish-brown, and iron sights are present.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular muzzle opening, perforated muzzle device, and the narrow profile of the wire stock.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the full length of the rifle, the box magazine, skeletonized stock, reddish-brown handguard, and iron sights.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the wire stock and the alignment of the iron sights along the top of the receiver.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same features as view 1, with more detail on the handguard and magazine attachment.
